channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - techraptor.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: Riot Games Previews New Cards for Upcoming Riftbound: Radiance Set
item.content:encoded:
Riot Games has released some new information on Riftbound: Radiance. The fifth upcoming set for their League of Legends card game will feature several beloved Champions from the beloved series across several products.

The Riftbound: Radiance Preview Announcement

Riot Games' preview of the Riftbound: Radiance set happened during the card game's Vancouver Regional Qualifier. In this broadcast, Riot mentioned that the theme of Radiance was around performance, both with the characters featured and the gameplay focus on big plays and big turns. Hopefully, the turns aren't too big to cause updates to the game's ban list.

The set's release also marks a milestone: the English, Simplified Chinese, and French versions of Riftbound are reaching release parity. This is important since releases were initially staggered between Chinese and English-speaking audiences at the start of the game's launch, and French language support only happened recently.

As for the cards shown off, there are a few standouts. Three out of the nine Champions featured in the set, Ekko, Evelynn, and Seraphine, all got card artwork revealed. Ekko, Ingenious, and Evelynn, In Control, simply had their portraits showcased. Whereas Seraphine, Not Alone, got a full card reveal, showcasing artwork, cost, and rules text.

Seraphine will also be featured in the Riftbound: Radiance Showdown deck. Like prior Showdown decks, the set will contain two, ready-to-play decks fresh out of the box. One will feature Seraphine; the other will feature Evelynn. 

The preview also confirms booster display boxes, featuring artwork of champions Ekko, Seraphine, Ziggs, and Jarvan, the Radiance Vault, containing six boosters, a rune pack, and three double-sided full art tokens, and Pre-Rift event kits featuring nine different champion precons.

Lastly, the Riftbound: Radiance preview did confirm several key dates. The set's preview season will start September 28th and end October 9th. Pre-Rift events will take place between October 16th and October 22nd. Finally, the set's global release will happen as previously announced on October 23, 2026. 

Ever since he was small, Tyler Chancey has had a deep, abiding love for video games and a tendency to think and overanalyze everything he enjoyed. This… More about Tyler
